title: Manifeste Canon: La Mémoire Inaltérable
sources: README.md: ROM Awakening and RAG infrastructure requirements [cite: 2]
quests.ts: Foundational Quest Specifications for District A
database.sql: Memo table schema for local PostgreSQL storage
forom-config.ts: Savoir/Canon RAG Data label mapping [cite: 3]
App.tsx: Quest reward and pixel economy implementation
resume: The quest "Manifeste Canon" (A8) is the definitive step for the Vision district, mapping to the "Savoir" action at coordinate A8 on the 10x10 FRM grid[cite: 3]. In the Forom architecture, the "Savoir" tag is specifically designated for "Canon RAG Data," which represents the highest tier of community knowledge intended for the future local AI assistant, ROM[cite: 2, 3]. While previous quests in the Vision district involved ideation, drafts, and debates, A8 serves as the finalized, immutable record that defines the community's mission: "Save human communities & long-term memory"[cite: 2]. This memo is a cornerstone of Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), where the goal is to establish the 100-memo core[cite: 2]. Technically, this data is stored in the local PostgreSQL "memos" table, which associates the entry with category "A" and question "8". This localized database is crucial for the "Minecraft Server" philosophy, ensuring that the community—not a third-party cloud—owns the physical keys to its history[cite: 2]. To support the eventual Phase 3 "Awakening," the system is designed to run on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson, which provides the necessary compute for local TensorRT/LLM RAG inference[cite: 2]. Every member who contributes to this canonized archive during Phase 1 receives a reward of 2 Pixels (PX), reinforcing the project's internal economy[cite: 2]. This reward system is vital for redistributing purchasing power, starting with the 500 PX held by each of the 9 Super-Mods[cite: 2]. The "Creator" role is specifically aligned with this quest, as their primary function is to produce high-quality content and build the tools that push the mission forward[cite: 2]. Once archived, this Manifeste Canon becomes a permanent part of the V1 Archive, set to be refined in Phase 2 and ultimately ingested by ROM once the community completes the required rituals and population checks[cite: 2]. This process ensures that knowledge transfer, especially in pilot projects like the ETS Clubs, is based on a verified, "canon" source of truth that prevents information decay over long periods[cite: 2]. By establishing this source at A8, the community provides a stable anchor for its spatial 5x5 viewport navigation, allowing for consistent discovery[cite: 2]. This ensures that any future user, or the AI itself, can immediately retrieve the community's fundamental identity. The Manifeste Canon at A8 is the structural guarantee that the forom's memory will remain intact for decades of service[cite: 2]. This vision is now sovereign, private, and powerful. It serves as a central benchmark.
summary: This quest represents the canonization of the community's vision as RAG-ready data at coordinate A8, serving as the immutable foundation for the future ROM AI.
